title: parse_zec_to_zatoshi rejects leading-dot notation with misleading error
description: core/src/amount.rs: parse_zec_to_zatoshi splits on '.' and parses the whole part. Input like '.5' produces whole="", which fails u64::parse with 'invalid amount' — not 'missing leading digit'. A user typing '.001' ZEC gets a confusing error. Fix: after split_once('.'), check that whole is non-empty before parsing, and return a specific error like 'amount must start with a digit (e.g. 0.001 not .001)'. Alternatively normalise: if whole.is_empty() { whole = "0" }. The second option is more user-friendly and consistent with JSON number parsing.
